# go

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Generated Go SDK for the OpenMeter v1 REST API. client.gen.go is fully generated by oapi-codegen from api/openapi.cloud.yaml; client.go adds ergonomic wrappers and auth helpers on top. A public SDK — must remain importable without the full monorepo.

## Patterns

**Never edit client.gen.go** — client.gen.go carries the oapi-codegen DO NOT EDIT header and is regenerated by make gen-api; add wrappers to client.go instead. (`// add wrapper method to client.go, not client.gen.go`)
**Ergonomic wrappers in client.go only** — client.go is the only hand-written Go file; add only methods that delegate to verbose generated method names — no type definitions or business logic. (`func (c *ClientWithResponses) IngestEventWithResponse(ctx, event, reqEditors...) (*IngestEventsResponse, error) { return c.IngestEventsWithApplicationCloudeventsPlusJSONBodyWithResponse(ctx, event, reqEditors...) }`)
**Auth via RequestEditorFn option** — NewAuthClient(WithResponses) prepend a WithRequestEditorFn that sets Authorization: Bearer, then append caller-supplied opts; auth is never embedded in generated code. (`WithRequestEditorFn(func(ctx, req *http.Request) error { req.Header.Set("Authorization", fmt.Sprintf("Bearer %s", apiSecret)); return nil })`)
**Tests use httptest.NewServer mock** — client_test.go validates each wrapper against an httptest.Server, asserting method, path, Content-Type, and comparing decoded structs with assert.Equal. (`server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w, r) { assert.Equal(t, http.MethodPost, r.Method); assert.Equal(t, "/api/v1/events", r.URL.Path) }))`)
**Enum Valid() for runtime validation** — Generated enums (AddonStatus, AppType, etc.) have a Valid() bool switch over all constants; use it instead of manual switches. (`func (e AppType) Valid() bool { switch e { case AppTypeCustomInvoicing, AppTypeSandbox, AppTypeStripe: return true; default: return false } }`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `client.gen.go` | Fully generated SDK: all types, enums, request/response structs, and HTTP methods from api/openapi.cloud.yaml via codegen.yaml. | Do not edit; regenerate with make gen-api. Uses always-prefix-enum-values and disable-required-readonly-as-pointer — changing codegen.yaml flags affects all enum names and pointer semantics. |
| `client.go` | Hand-written ergonomic layer: auth constructors (NewAuthClient(WithResponses)) and CloudEvents ingest shorthands (IngestEvent/Batch + WithResponse variants); holds the //go:generate directive. | Only add methods delegating to generated methods — no type definitions or business logic. |
| `codegen.yaml` | oapi-codegen config: package 'openmeter', generates client + models + embedded-spec into client.gen.go with always-prefix-enum-values. | Changing compatibility flags affects all enum constant names and pointer semantics across the SDK; coordinate with api/openapi.cloud.yaml changes. |
| `client_test.go` | White-box (package openmeter) unit tests for the hand-written wrappers using mock HTTP servers; imports openmeter/meter for MeterAggregation casting. | TetsListEvents (note the typo) is never run by go test — the missing Test prefix means the function is silently skipped; do not replicate the pattern. |
| `error.go` | ErrResponse struct (StatusCode, StatusText, AppCode, Message) for rendering HTTP error responses. | Separate from generated error types in client.gen.go; ErrResponse is for rendering, not domain errors. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Editing client.gen.go manually — overwritten on every make gen-api run
- Adding new API types or request/response structs to client.go — they belong in api/spec/ TypeSpec
- Embedding auth tokens/credentials directly in client methods instead of ClientOption/RequestEditorFn
- Importing app-internal monorepo packages beyond those already referenced — this is a public SDK
- Writing tests that bypass httptest.NewServer and make live HTTP calls

## Decisions

- **Generated from api/openapi.cloud.yaml with always-prefix-enum-values** — The cloud variant includes cloud-specific endpoints; enum prefixing prevents collisions when enums share value strings (e.g. 'active').
- **Ergonomic wrappers in client.go rather than custom codegen templates** — CloudEvents ingest has two content-types producing verbose generated names; short wrappers improve DX without forking the generator.
- **Auth injected via RequestEditorFn option chain** — Keeps generated code immutable and lets callers compose multiple editors (auth + tracing + retry) without modifying SDK source.

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
